observation: The current env_bootstrap probes only languages, package managers, and memory. All 23 failing trace files have empty `steps` arrays and carry only an `error_summary`. By stratum: scientific-computing 0.375 (worst non-zero), video-processing 0.0, data-science 0.571, machine-learning 0.667. Failing tasks in these strata (raman-fitting, dna-insert, protein-assembly, dna-assembly, adaptive-rejection-sampler, caffe-cifar-10, torch-pipeline-parallelism, sam-cell-seg, video-processing) all need information the bootstrap never provides: (1) which scientific Python packages are pre-installed, (2) GPU availability, (3) extra CLI tools like ffmpeg. torch-pipeline-parallelism failed with ModuleNotFoundError for 'pipeline_parallel' at step 21, indicating the agent spent steps discovering environment instead of writing the solution. gpt2-codegolf and make-doom-for-mips hit the 2700s timeout — knowledge of pre-installed packages and GPU could allow more direct strategies.
hypothesis: Agents in scientific-computing, ML, and video-processing tasks waste multiple early steps probing for GPU, Python package availability, and tools like ffmpeg. If env_bootstrap surfaces these upfront (GPU spec, pre-installed packages like scipy/biopython/torch, and extra CLI tools like ffmpeg/git/make), the agent can immediately choose the correct strategy without exploration turns, converting some step-budget failures into passes.
change_summary: Added three new bootstrap sections: @@GPU@@ (nvidia-smi for GPU name+VRAM), @@DISK@@ (df -h /app for available disk), @@PYLIB@@ (python3 importlib.util check for 20 common scientific/ML packages: numpy, scipy, matplotlib, pandas, sklearn, torch, tensorflow, biopython, Bio, cv2, PIL, ffmpeg, playwright, statsmodels, seaborn, sympy, networkx, requests, lxml, h5py, numba), and @@XTOOLS@@ (ffmpeg, git, make, mips-linux-gnu-gcc, curl, wget). Timeout extended from 15s/20s to 20s/25s to accommodate extra checks. New sections are rendered in the snapshot only when they contain useful info (GPU omits 'not available' line when no GPU, PYLIB omits when empty, XTOOLS omits 'not found' entries).
